top_stories.title: SailPoint to Acquire Entro in Reported $200 Million Deal
top_stories.summary: SailPoint is acquiring Israel-based Entro Security in a reported $200 million deal. Entro specializes in non-human identity and secrets security, and the acquisition will expand SailPoint's identity governance capabilities into the NHI (non-human identity) space.

top_stories.title: What is new in Microsoft Entra: June 2026 updates
top_stories.summary: Microsoft Entra's June 2026 update introduces phishing-resistant MFA for Linux desktops, expanded passkey support on Windows, and changes to Self-Service Password Reset identity verification. The update also includes new Conditional Access enforcement changes.

top_stories.title: IBM and Microsoft partner to automate identity threat response
top_stories.summary: IBM and Microsoft have launched a collaborative service that integrates Microsoft's security telemetry with IBM's managed response services to automate identity threat detection and remediation. The partnership is designed to bridge the gap between identifying identity-based threats and executing governed remediation actions.

top_stories.title: Microsoft enhances Entra and Defender to counter AI-driven identity attacks
top_stories.summary: Microsoft has enhanced its Entra and Defender products to counter AI-driven identity attacks. The updates address threats where attackers use AI to automate reconnaissance, personalize social engineering, and chain vulnerabilities against identity systems.

top_stories.title: Novo Nordisk Breach Highlights Software Development Pipeline Risk
top_stories.summary: A security breach at Novo Nordisk involved a leaked GitHub token that exposed vulnerabilities in secrets management within software development pipelines. The incident is framed as an identity problem rather than a tooling problem, highlighting the need for organizations to apply identity governance principles to machine identities and developer credentials.

news.title: FortiBleed campaign used custom FortiGate sniffer to steal credentials
news.summary: A large-scale campaign dubbed "FortiBleed" targeted Fortinet FortiGate devices using custom sniffers to harvest authentication secrets and steal credentials from compromised firewalls. SOCRadar identified the attack, which specifically targeted authentication data stored on network security appliances.

news.title: A Glimpse into the “Search Your Target” Market for Stolen Credentials
news.summary: Flare has published research examining an emerging underground market where attackers can pay to search stolen credential databases for specific companies, domains, and accounts. The research highlights how threat actors can now target specific organizations' credentials without manually sifting through large credential dumps.

news.title: Hundreds of AI-powered iOS apps found exposing credentials
news.summary: Researchers from Wake Forest University analyzed 444 iOS applications with LLM features and found 282 exposing exploitable credentials or backend access mechanisms. The study covered apps across 13 categories, highlighting widespread failures in securing API credentials and backend access controls in AI-powered mobile applications.

news.title: 29-Year-Old Squid Proxy Bug 'Squidbleed' Can Leak Cleartext HTTP Requests
news.summary: A 29-year-old heap over-read vulnerability dubbed "Squidbleed" in the Squid web proxy can leak cleartext HTTP requests, including credentials and session tokens, to other users sharing the same proxy. The bug originates from a 1997 FTP-parsing change and remains present in Squid's default configuration.

news.title: Navigating the AI access control minefield
news.summary: The article addresses access control challenges for AI systems requiring enterprise data access, framing it as an emerging IAM problem domain. It draws parallels to early e-commerce adoption to contextualize the need for secure, governed AI access management.

news.title: 生成AI型AI-OCR「GenOCR」、SAML 認証および Microsoft Entra ID による SSO に対応 ― ユーザーのセキュリティ強化と利便性向上へ
news.summary: GenOCR, a generative AI-based OCR product by Jinbei, has added support for SAML authentication and SSO via Microsoft Entra ID. The update aims to improve security and convenience for users of the document recognition platform.

news.title: Show HN: Lelu – authorization engine that catches manipulated AI agents
news.summary: An open source authorization engine called Lelu has been released on GitHub, designed specifically for AI agents with features including confidence-aware gating, human-in-the-loop review, policy-as-code, and audit trails. The project is hosted under the lelu-auth organization and targets the problem of manipulated or compromised AI agents requiring authorization controls.

news.title: Klue OAuth breach victim list grows as Icarus hackers claim attack
news.summary: Market intelligence platform Klue suffered a security breach in which threat actors stole OAuth tokens used to connect to customers' Salesforce environments. The attack was claimed by a new extortion group called "Icarus," and the victim list is reportedly growing.

news.title: Every AI Agent Is an Identity. Most Organizations Don't Treat Them That Way
news.summary: Token Security has published an analysis arguing that AI agents represent a new class of identity requiring governance, as they can access data, trigger workflows, deploy code, and interact with critical business systems with limited oversight. The piece frames AI agents as an emerging identity and access management challenge, linking agent credentials and permissions to existing IAM and governance frameworks.

news.title: Webinar: How attackers bypass MFA and how defenders can respond
news.summary: A webinar addresses how modern phishing techniques, including Device Code phishing, can bypass MFA protections to gain unauthorized access to corporate accounts. The session covers behavioral AI-based detection methods and automated response workflows as countermeasures for security teams.

news.title: Forget Data Leakage: Shadow AI's Real Threat Is Access Control
news.summary: The article argues that shadow AI has evolved from a data leakage issue into an access control problem, with the core risk now centered on unauthorized AI agents and tools gaining inappropriate access to enterprise systems. The piece reframes AI security concerns around identity and access management principles, positioning access control as the primary defense against unsanctioned AI use.

news.title: Salesforce Disables Klue App Integration After OAuth Token Abuse Exposes Customer Data
news.summary: Salesforce disabled the Klue Battlecards app integration after a security incident on June 11, 2026, involving OAuth token abuse that exposed customer data. The incident resulted in organizations being unable to connect to Salesforce via the Klue app until further notice, highlighting risks associated with OAuth-based third-party integrations.

news.title: FIFA Bug Exposes World Cup Streams to Remote Takeover
news.summary: A security vulnerability in FIFA's systems allowed potential remote takeover of World Cup broadcast streams due to unenforced Microsoft Entra access controls. The bug exposed how misconfigured identity and access management controls could have enabled unauthorized actors to manipulate live streaming infrastructure.

news.title: Orphaned AI Agents: How to Find Hidden Access Risks Inside Your Network
news.summary: The article addresses the emerging IAM challenge of orphaned AI agents that retain standing privileges after their creators leave an organization. It focuses on the governance and access management risks posed by autonomous AI tools operating without clear authorization accountability within enterprise environments.

news.title: Zero-Touch OAuth for MCP
news.summary: The Model Context Protocol (MCP) has released a stable Enterprise-Managed Authorization extension that enables organizations to provision MCP server access centrally through their identity provider. The extension eliminates per-app OAuth flows by automatically connecting users to authorized MCP servers upon first login, integrating with existing IdP infrastructure.

news.title: Google Toldl Researchers 'Nice Catch!' Then Denied Bug Bounty For Flaw It Still Hasn't Fixed
news.summary: Security researcher Justin O'Leary discovered a privilege escalation vulnerability in Google's Config Connector that Google initially accepted as high-severity before denying a bug bounty by reclassifying it as "working as intended." The flaw relates to privilege escalation, an access control concern, but remains unfixed and the dispute centers on Google's bug bounty program process rather than a broad IAM platform issue.

news.title: Microsoft Entra ID single sign-on for the Starburst connector in Power BI (Preview)
news.summary: Microsoft has released a preview feature enabling Entra ID single sign-on for the Starburst connector in Power BI. The integration allows report viewers querying semantic models in DirectQuery mode to authenticate to Starburst using their own Entra ID identity, enforcing Starburst's row-level and column-level access policies.

news.title: Semperis Appoints Jimmy McNary as Deputy Federal CTO to Drive Identity Security Strategy Across the Public Sector
news.summary: Semperis has appointed Jimmy McNary as Deputy Federal CTO to lead identity security strategy for its public sector division. McNary is a cybersecurity veteran with over 25 years of experience, previously serving as VP of Federal Solutions.

news.title: Sweeping Credential-Harvesting Heist Compromises 30K+ Fortinet Devices
news.summary: A large-scale credential-harvesting campaign has compromised over 30,000 Fortinet devices across nearly 200 countries. Attackers have compiled a list of working credentials from the compromised devices, actively targeting organizations across various sectors.

news.title: Hackers Used Meta’s AI Support Bot to Seize Instagram Accounts
news.summary: Hackers exploited Meta's AI support assistant chatbot to bypass account authentication and reset passwords, successfully compromising high-profile Instagram accounts including the Obama White House and U.S. Space Force Chief Master Sergeant. Instructions for the attack circulated on Telegram, demonstrating how AI-powered support tools can be manipulated to circumvent identity verification and account recovery controls.

news.title: Lawmakers Demand Answers as CISA Tries to Contain Data Leak
news.summary: A CISA contractor intentionally published AWS GovCloud keys and other agency secrets to a public GitHub account, causing a credentials breach that CISA is still working to contain. Congressional lawmakers from both chambers are demanding answers from the agency as it works to invalidate the leaked credentials.

news.title: CISA Admin Leaked AWS GovCloud Keys on Github
news.summary: A CISA contractor exposed highly privileged AWS GovCloud account credentials and internal system access details in a public GitHub repository until this past weekend. The leaked files included information on how CISA builds, tests, and deploys software internally, representing a significant government credential exposure incident.
